conclusion: The frozen identical-source control, one-pair A/B smoke, and six-pair three-map side-swap panel all passed under the exact persisted runner. The final panel completed 6/6 semantic-exact paired ranked replays with complete production/capture traces, exact chosen-decision and semantic counter-delta traces, one driver per match, inner/outer/counter parity, and no safety event or failed outer search-state restore.

scope.caveat: n=6 paired matches is sufficient for this focused operational replay gate, not for latency percentile qualification or a performance-improvement claim. Baseline and candidate workers ran sequentially on one host, decisions within a match are correlated, and symmetric v0.8 side swaps provide operational coverage rather than independent roster scenarios.

thresholds.evidence: 300 was imported from the archived server BOT_SEARCH_CIRCUIT_BREAKER_MS. Every constructed archived-common SearchDriver was inspected at runtime and asserted to expose decisionDeadlineMs=175 and circuitBreakerMs=275.

protocol.setup: SEE_NONE + unchanged ranked setup policy + deterministic random Tier-1/Tier-2 artifacts
protocol.processIsolation: The coordinator launched a fresh sequential Bun worker per variant. A Bun resolver redirected every archived server @heroesofcrypto/common import to exactly one immutable common archive.
protocol.deterministicCrypto: per-match deterministic crypto.randomUUID + crypto.getRandomValues reset; identical paired gameplay randomness and unit/summon ids without production edits

semanticCapture.journal: Uncapped ordered accepted actions with parsed actionJson and complete GameEvent arrays from eventsJson.
semanticCapture.serverEvents: Uncapped complete server IPlayEvent stream, including snapshots and journal entries.
semanticCapture.state: Authoritative snapshotBattle state plus selected server semantic state after every accepted fight action; every state is hashed even when full state payload retention is disabled.
semanticCapture.decision: Every inner chosen decision, semantic search-counter delta, outer chosen decision, and inner/outer ordinal is retained and hashed.
semanticCapture.drainSchedule: drain retained journal/events after creation, every publication, and every manager tick

included: complete semantic snapshotBattle common engine state (units/order/grid/fight/holder/AI target memory)
server unitMetadata and sceneLog explicitly restored by the production wrapper
excluded: SearchDriver counters and circuit flag, which intentionally mutate as observability/safety state
console output, which is append-only observability rather than restorable match state
external crypto RNG cursor, which production does not expose or restore
server session fields not read or mutated by searchBotDecision
restoreValidation.scope: This is the complete authoritative restorable common state exposed by snapshotBattle plus damage/search-stable state and the server metadata/log state the wrapper explicitly restores; it is not a byte-for-byte snapshot of the entire PlaySession object

protocol.timing: innerChooseDecisionLatencyMs times real SearchDriver.chooseDecision. outerWrapperLatencyMs surrounds the complete private searchBotDecision call, including metadata snapshot and final restore. The unchanged server circuit flag/log is authoritative for the production 300 ms breaker. Percentiles use nearest-rank ceil(q*n)-1 over all decision calls.

policy.callerIdentity: The runner rejects missing, unknown, or mismatched caller common/server SHA labels before worker launch.
policy.preflight: Before launch it recursively seals every file under both common src archives and the complete archived server src tree, validates the frozen expected manifests and exact A/B delta, and seals package/lock inputs.
policy.postflight: After both workers finish it recomputes the same trees and package/lock inputs; any mismatch aborts result emission.

dependencies.limitation: package.json and resolved bun.lock files are sealed, but the symlinked node_modules file contents are not recursively sealed; lock/install drift remains outside this source-equivalence proof
package.json and resolved bun.lock files are sealed, but the symlinked node_modules file contents are not recursively sealed; lock/install drift remains outside this source-equivalence proof

sourceIntegrity.dependencyLimitation: package.json and resolved bun.lock files are sealed, but the symlinked node_modules file contents are not recursively sealed; lock/install drift remains outside this source-equivalence proof

passContract: Every fight must finish, productionReplayComplete and captureComplete must be true, and uncapped replay capture must be complete.

Journal, GameEvent, server-event semantic stream, per-action state trace, final state, chosen-decision trace, and semantic counter-delta trace must be exact; the first chosen-decision and counter-delta difference ordinals must be null.
Each match and variant must construct exactly one SearchDriver; inner decisions, outer wrapper calls, and semantic counter decision deltas must have equal counts.
searched plus singleCandidate must equal total decision calls for every match and variant; illegalIncumbent must match between variants.
Proposal rejects, server errors, deadline fallbacks, circuit skips/openings/warnings, and unrestored outer decisions must all be zero.

identicalSourceControl.purpose: The baseline archive was supplied as both variants; the runner required the identical-source zero delta and baseline frozen manifest.

candidateSmoke.purpose: One native-side baseline/candidate pair using the exact persisted final runner.

durableRaw.losslessVerification: gzip -t passed; gzip -cd piped to shasum -a 256 and wc -c reproduced the raw SHA-256 and byte count exactly.

threeMapSideSwapPanel.perMatchInvariant: All six comparisons were exact, complete, and production-complete; both variants had one driver and equal inner/outer/counter counts. Every per-match safety field was zero/false, every restorable-state before/after hash matched, and the first chosen-decision and counter-delta difference ordinals were null.

knownLimits: This is a focused three-map operational gate, not the planned 420-session (210-pair) latency panel and not a statistical latency qualification.

The common workspace/server locks and package manifests are sealed, but the symlinked installed node_modules contents are not recursively sealed; dependency install drift remains outside the source-equivalence proof.
All six production retained streams remained below 512 entries, so the uncapped drain path ran but the production retention cap was not crossed.
The deterministic crypto substitution exists only inside isolated replay workers. It proves paired semantics; it does not reproduce unpredictability of live secure randomness.
Physical side swaps are included for operational coverage even though symmetric v0.8 self-play does not double the number of independent roster scenarios.
